overview.Summary: The threat actor C0032 targets transportation systems using a simple attack chain involving spear-phishing, PowerShell, and disabling security software.

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shadow IT, Poor implementation of access controls, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Web, Phishing, Vulnerability exploit, Malware, Account takeover, Brute-force attack, Distributed Denial of Service(DDoS)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2017-12231 [CWE-22]: Microsoft Office memory corruption vulnerability
stage.description: The threat actor sends a phishing email with a malicious attachment exploiting a vulnerability in Microsoft Office to gain initial access to the target's network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.kev: CVE-2023-6549 [CWE-476]: Windows PowerShell remoting service remote code execution vulnerability
stage.description: The threat actor exploits a vulnerability in PowerShell to execute arbitrary commands on the compromised system, enabling further lateral movement within the network.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The threat actor disables security software to avoid detection while moving laterally and exfiltrating sensitive data.

Scenario.summary: The threat actor successfully gains initial access, executes malicious payloads, and disables security software to evade detection and move laterally within the transportation systems' network.
